description: The observatory has just installed a new field flattener lens. Characterize the sensor's optical vignetting and read noise by capturing 5 BIAS frames (0s exposure) to ~/Calibration/Bias/ and 5 FLAT frames (2s exposure, 'Luminance' filter in Slot 1) to ~/Calibration/Flats/. Create a Python script at ~/analyze_sensor.py that takes a FITS file path as a command-line argument and uses astropy.io.fits and numpy to calculate the Vignetting Ratio (mean of the four 50x50 extreme corners combined [[:50,:50], [:50,-50:], [-50:,:50], [-50:,-50:]] divided by the mean of the central 100x100 pixels [H//2-50:H//2+50, W//2-50:W//2+50]) and the RMS Noise (standard deviation of the entire image array). The script must print exactly two lines to standard output: "Vignetting Ratio: <float>" and "RMS Noise: <float>". Save the combined analysis results from one Bias frame and one Flat frame to ~/Documents/sensor_report.txt.
